findings.title: MCP tool description injection detected

findings.title: Scope creep - accessing undeclared resources
findings.description: Component instructs agent to access files, APIs, or systems beyond the scope declared in its manifest.

toxic_flows.title: Tool Poisoning + Exfiltration Chain
toxic_flows.description: Component poisons tool descriptions AND exfiltrates data. The tool poisoning hijacks agent behavior, while the exfil instructions transmit the stolen data - a silent harvest chain.
toxic_flows.remediation: 1. Remove all behavioral instructions from tool descriptions. 2. Remove all data transmission instructions. 3. Scan with bawbel scan-server-card before connecting any MCP server.

toxic_flows.title: Scope Expansion + Exfiltration Chain
toxic_flows.description: Component expands its declared scope to access undeclared resources AND exfiltrates data. Accesses more than declared, transmits the excess - a scope creep + exfiltration chain.
toxic_flows.remediation: 1. Remove all undeclared resource access instructions. 2. Remove all data transmission instructions. 3. Declare all required permissions explicitly in the component manifest.

findings.title: Exfiltration via tool output encoding
findings.description: Component instructs the agent to encode sensitive data inside tool call parameters or return values for covert exfiltration.

findings.title: AVE_MultiTurnAttack
findings.description: YARA rule matched

toxic_flows.title: Persistence + Data Exfiltration Chain
toxic_flows.description: Component establishes persistence AND exfiltrates data. The persistence ensures the exfiltration continues across sessions and context resets - a long-running data harvest chain.
toxic_flows.remediation: 1. Remove all persistence instructions. 2. Remove all data transmission instructions. 3. Scan all startup scripts and cron entries for injected instructions.

toxic_flows.title: Goal Override + Exfiltration Chain
toxic_flows.description: Component overrides agent goals AND exfiltrates data. The override disables safety constraints, the exfil transmits whatever the agent can access - a combined hijack + harvest chain.
toxic_flows.remediation: 1. Remove all goal override instructions. 2. Remove all data transmission instructions.

toxic_flows.title: Covert Channel + Persistence Chain
toxic_flows.description: Component uses a covert channel (steganography, timing) to exfiltrate data AND establishes persistence. The covert channel evades detection, the persistence ensures long-term access - a stealthy harvest chain.
toxic_flows.remediation: 1. Remove all steganographic encoding or covert channel instructions. 2. Remove all persistence instructions. 3. Audit agent outputs for encoded data using forensic tooling.

findings.title: MCP server impersonation or spoofing
findings.description: Component falsely claims to be an official or trusted MCP server to gain elevated trust or permissions from the agent.

findings.title: Cryptocurrency drain pattern detected
findings.description: Component instructs agent to transfer cryptocurrency or interact with wallets in ways that suggest a drain attack.

toxic_flows.title: Credential Exfiltration Chain
toxic_flows.description: Component reads credentials or secrets AND transmits data externally. Complete credential theft attack chain - reads API keys, .env files, or tokens, then encodes and exfiltrates them to an attacker-controlled endpoint.
toxic_flows.remediation: 1. Remove all credential-read patterns - agent should never instruct the model to read .env, API keys, or tokens. 2. Remove all external transmission instructions. 3. If both cannot be removed, isolate them into separate components with no shared execution context.

toxic_flows.title: Credential Exfiltration Chain
toxic_flows.description: Component reads credentials or secrets AND transmits data externally. Complete credential theft attack chain - reads API keys, .env files, or tokens, then encodes and exfiltrates them to an attacker-controlled endpoint.
toxic_flows.remediation: 1. Remove all credential-read patterns - agent should never instruct the model to read .env, API keys, or tokens. 2. Remove all external transmission instructions. 3. If both cannot be removed, isolate them into separate components with no shared execution context.
